content:
Write better prompts. Build better solutions.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Berke Citak on UnsplashI used to think the problem was the AI.
Every time it generated mediocre code, skipped important details, or completely misunderstood what I wanted, I blamed the model.
I tried different tools. Different chatbots. Different settings.
Nothing consistently worked.
Then one afternoon, while building a small Python automation script, I realized something that completely changed how I use AI.
The quality of the output wasn’t limited by the model.
It was limited by the instructions I gave it.
That realization has probably saved me hundreds of hours over the past year.
If you’re learning Python, building automation tools, or experimenting with AI, this single shift in mindset can improve almost every response you get.
You’re usually just one prompt away from a dramatically better answer.
The Biggest Mistake I See Developers Make
When most people use AI, they ask questions like this:
Write a Python script to organize files.
